title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: scoring scheme scores linear map standardized observed variables, S = Z W, -level score correlations exactly: scores need materialized — R weight matrices. Waller (2007) derived orthogonal components, identity holds linear W, includes regression (Thurstone, W = R^{-1} Λ), Bartlett, tenBerge scores. standard PCA EFA/ESEM (regression-scored) paths algebra-eligible. Standardization trap. Component/factor scores unit-variance general (Bartlett oblique scores especially). Always divide real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)). Assuming unit variance silently corrupts correlations.
code: cor(S_a, S_b) = D_a^{-1/2} (W_a' R W_b) D_b^{-1/2}            where  R   = input correlation matrix (pearson or polychoric)                   D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x)   # score variances; NOT assumed 1

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Two situations force scores route: 1. Nonlinear scoring — EAP/MAP/ML scores categorical/ordinal ESEM. (ordinal ESEM instead uses regression scores polychoric loadings, stays linear → algebra polychoric R.) 2. User wants empirical, sample-realized correlations (including FIML/missing-data realities) rather model-implied quantity. missingness differ; document one “edge” represents (default: model-consistent / algebra eligible).
code: compute_edges(levels, R,               edge_method = c("auto", "algebra", "scores"),               pairs  = c("adjacent", "all"),   # "all" for Forbes extension               data   = NULL,               align  = TRUE,               use    = "pairwise") {    for (each pair (a, b) in chosen pairs) {      algebra_ok <- levels[[a]]$scoring$linear &&                   levels[[b]]$scoring$linear && !is.null(R)      if (edge_method == "algebra" || (edge_method == "auto" && algebra_ok)) {       Wa <- levels[[a]]$scoring$weights       Wb <- levels[[b]]$scoring$weights       C  <- crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wb)                       # W_a' R W_b       sa <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wa)))           # score SDs       sb <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wb, R %*% Wb)))       E  <- sweep(sweep(C, 1, sa, "/"), 2, sb, "/")       # standardize     } else {       stopifnot(!is.null(data))                           # nonlinear/EAP or edge_method="scores"       Sa <- score(levels[[a]], data); Sb <- score(levels[[b]], data)       E  <- cor(Sa, Sb, use = use)     }      if (align) E <- align_signs(E, lineage)               # see §7     edges[[pair]] <- E   }   # return: list of (k_a x k_b) matrices  +  tidy edge tibble (from, to, r, is_primary, above_cut) }

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: IDs: models numbered factor count (m1…mK); factors within model m{k}f{j}. Deterministic within-level order = primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak. Sort factors within level (primary parent’s position level , descending variance). single top factor ties fall back variance. places child parent splits read cleanly; f{j} ID order follows layout order tables plots agree. Limitation: ordering reduces can’t eliminate crossings (child strong secondary parent elsewhere still throws crossing edge); layout’s barycenter step (§11) refines x-position continuously beyond discrete order. Lineage lives edges/lineage, never ID. child can multiple strong parents (point method), don’t encode parentage m{k}f{j} — breaks structure isn’t clean tree. Matching: assign factor primary parent via greedy per-column argmax |r| (.e. apply(abs(E), 2, .max)). Multiple children sharing parent normal expected — adjacent levels always n_b = n_a + 1 (level k k factors vs k−1 ), pigeonhole least two children share parent. bijection (LSAP/Hungarian) therefore ill-posed removed. Tucker’s φ serves supplementary congruence criterion redundancy_phi filtering (Forbes extension, §14.19) used matching . Sign alignment — anchor primary parent, “positive.” Sign one DoF per factor; make every cross-level correlation positive. Rule: anchor m1f1 defined orientation (positive sum loadings, substantive marker item), orient factor correlation primary parent positive, propagating top-. Document non-primary edges may legitimately negative.

title: DESIGN.md — Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Resolved (design round): 1. Rotation → varimax (orthogonal) across engines, hardcoded internal constant since M13; oblique rotation scope (resolved 2026-06 — confounds cross-level signal method’s whole point). rotation removed user argument M13. 2. Correlation basis → pearson default, polychoric opt-, ordinal-detection cli warning (silent switching). 3. Within-level order → primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak; f{j} IDs follow . 4. Ordinal ESEM scoring → tenBerge polychoric (linear → algebra) default; EAP opt-. Default Likert path need data edge time. 5. Package name → ackwards (verify via available::available("ackwards")). Resolved build (M1–M3): 6. Ordinal-detection heuristic: ≤ 7 distinct integer values triggers warning. 7. CF kappa: κ = 1/p (varimax-equivalent; Crawford & Ferguson 1970). kappa argument accepted stored never wired engine — engines hardcoded cfT → "varimax". Removed entirely M13: CF(κ=1/p) ≡ varimax; literature never varies kappa; exposing implied quartimax/equamax reasonable alternatives (method). 8. cor = "spearman" added alongside "pearson" non-polychoric rank-based option. 9. fm argument exposed EFA engine ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). 10. cut_show argument exposed (default 0.3) control edges flagged above_cut. Resolved M4: 11. ESEM engine → lavaan::efa() (EFA--SEM, full ESEM structural paths). Gives per-level fit indices + rotation-aware SEs + WLSMV ordinal estimation. require full ESEM block syntax; bass-ackwards levels independent EFA solutions. 12. ESEM scoring → self-compute tenBerge weights lavaan’s estimated loadings Λ latent correlation matrix R via existing .tenBerge_weights(R, Λ). lavPredict() used default path (lacks tenBerge); eventual hook EAP opt-. 13. EAP scoring → deferred; EAP request returns cli_abort("yet implemented"). scores-route seam compute_edges() preserved EAP lands. 14. cor = "polychoric" → general basis engines (ESEM-). PCA/EFA compute R via psych::polychoric() ackwards() feed engine usual. ESEM uses lavaan’s latent correlation matrix R edges (mix psych-polychoric R lavaan-WLSMV loadings). 15. ESEM ordinal estimator → WLSMV default; ULSMV documented option. 16. loadings_se → added §4 level contract (p×k matrix, NULL PCA/EFA). Completed M4 (addition items 11–16 ): - NPD polychoric matrices: warn + smooth via psych::cor.smooth() (implemented). - Convergence truncation tested ESEM engine (k=4+ p=6 triggers lavaan error → warn + truncate → object builds k_eff=3). - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check documented Pearson/continuous paths ; polychoric ESEM edges (algebra uses lavaan polychoric R; scores route uses Pearson standardization) diverge design excluded oracle. Resolved M5 (Forbes extension): 17. API → two orthogonal args: pairs = c("adjacent","") prune = c("none","redundant","artefact") (char vector; default "none"). prune != "none" auto-upgrades pairs "" loud cli_inform() (chains need -levels edges assess). 18. Prune action → flag-, never remove. Adds pruned/prune_reason annotation columns edge/node tidy structures; object retains levels (preserves Invariant 5 algebra-vs-scores oracle). Pruning interpretive relabeling, re-estimation — say print/docs (extends §2 honesty caveat). 19. Redundancy → faithful Forbes (2023): score-correlation chains |r| ≥ .9 (default, tunable), retention rule = keep bottom node chain reaches level k (-specific, best-defined), else keep topmost node (broadest manifestation). Tucker’s φ (> .95, Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006) computed aligned loadings optional conjunctive criterion. φ formula Σaᵢbᵢ / sqrt(Σaᵢ² · Σbᵢ²); base R, new dependency. 20. Additive enrichments paper (default output still matches Forbes’s examples): always report r φ every redundancy candidate (report-first, flag-second — borderline cases like paper’s .89/.93 alcohol component stay visible); report endpoint r (direct, -levels edges) alongside chain flag disagree (correlation non-transitive: clean adjacent chain neither implies implied endpoint identity — chain answers “perpetuates every level,” endpoint r answers “construct”). 21. Artefact → never auto-flagged. prune = "artefact" surfaces φ inspection; removal documented researcher judgment (Forbes explicit introduces researcher DoF / confirmation bias; cf. Wicherts et al. 2016). Known limitations / deferred future milestones: - factor_cor ESEM engine permuted variance-sort ord vector. Safe permanently: orthogonal rotation supported (factor_cor = ; permutation ), oblique rotation scope (§9, §14.1). guard comment engine_esem.R documents required decision ever reversed. - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check cover cor = "polychoric" paths (see ). - cor = "spearman" + engine = "esem" semantically inconsistent (lavaan fits Pearson ML raw data edges use Spearman R); warning now emitted (M10). - ESEM engine detect warn improper/Heywood solutions (lavaan negative residual variances), unlike EFA engine warns explicitly. - Forbes-extension improvements deferred past M5 (published method weaker spots worth strengthening later; M5 ships faithful method + §14.20 reporting enrichments): - Structural artefact signals. Beyond congruence, artefacts detectable structural signatures — split--merge pattern (indicators split level k reunite different parent k+1; Forbes Fig. 2), factors < 3 primary-loading items, orphaned/non-replicating factors. Surface diagnostics rather relying φ alone. - Factor-score-indeterminacy caveat EFA/ESEM redundancy. Score-correlation-based redundancy exact PCA (Waller algebra) inherits factor-score indeterminacy EFA/ESEM; φ (loading-based) stable criterion . Consider making φ default non-PCA engines. - Selection bias “strongest” edge. Plotting max correlation across many -levels pairs capitalizes chance (85 → 1,320 correlations levels grow). Add bootstrap CIs / SEs edges (reuse loadings_se infrastructure) strongest-edge claim inferentially honest.

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: PCA extracts principal components — linear combinations observed variables capture maximum variance, including measurement error. Every item modeled communality 1.0: components account 100% item’s variance. true latent variable model; data reduction method. bass-ackwards context, PCA natural default. fast, always converges, produces eigenvalues can guide choice k. Waller (2007) showed -level algebra (W'RW) holds exactly components, making edges algebraically exact rather approximated materialized scores. “fit” PCA just eigenvalue component — amount variance captures. chi-square tests, RMSEA, model rejection.

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM

what: prune = "artefact": factors defined by structural similarity
title: The Forbes Extension: Skip-Level Connections and Pruning
text: artefact factor one whose loading pattern similar factor non-adjacent level -level neighbors. Similarity measured Tucker’s congruence coefficient (φ): ϕ(Fa,Fb)=∑iλiaλib∑iλia2⋅∑iλib2\phi(F_a, F_b) = \frac{\sum_i \lambda_{ia}\lambda_{ib}} {\sqrt{\sum_i \lambda_{ia}^2 \cdot \sum_i \lambda_{ib}^2}} φ ranges −1 +1, values > 0.95 indicating near-identical loading patterns regardless sign. factor k = 3 φ > 0.95 factor k = 1 suspected artefact rotation rather genuine new dimension. BFI, artefact criterion flags nothing — factor level loading pattern similar factor non-adjacent level -level solution. good result well-validated instrument: suggests rotation producing genuinely distinct factors level, recycling old ones. data weaker noisier factor structure, rotations struggle separate highly correlated factors, artefact criterion often flag nodes. node can flagged one criterion ; two criteria capture different flavors redundancy: "redundant": factor appears multiple levels near-identical score correlations — persists unchanged k increases. "artefact": factor’s loading pattern closely resembles factor different, non-adjacent level — looks like copy rather refinement.
code: x_art <- ackwards(bfi,   k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric",   pairs = "all", prune = "artefact" ) #> ℹ Artefact mode: Tucker's computed for all cross-level factor pairs. #> ℹ Inspect `x$prune$phi` to identify potential artefacts; removal is a #>   researcher judgment (Forbes, 2023). tidy(x_art, what = "nodes") #>      id level pruned prune_reason #> 1  m1f1     1  FALSE         <NA> #> 2  m2f1     2  FALSE         <NA> #> 3  m2f2     2  FALSE         <NA> #> 4  m3f1     3  FALSE         <NA> #> 5  m3f2     3  FALSE         <NA> #> 6  m3f3     3  FALSE         <NA> #> 7  m4f1     4  FALSE         <NA> #> 8  m4f2     4  FALSE         <NA> #> 9  m4f3     4  FALSE         <NA> #> 10 m4f4     4  FALSE         <NA> #> 11 m5f1     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 12 m5f2     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 13 m5f3     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 14 m5f4     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 15 m5f5     5  FALSE         <NA>

text: redundancy criterion adjustable redundancy_r threshold (default 0.90) matching Forbes (2023). artefact criterion auto-flag threshold — prune = "artefact" computes Tucker’s φ researcher inspection; factors auto-flagged. BFI, result across wide range thresholds redundant chains correlations > 0.97 — flagging unambiguous. data may find borderline cases threshold matters: BFI thresholds agree: flagged factors robustly redundant, borderline cases. noisier datasets smaller samples typically see count increase lower threshold.
code: # Check how many factors are flagged at different redundancy_r thresholds thresholds <- c(0.80, 0.85, 0.90, 0.95) counts <- sapply(thresholds, function(thr) {   x <- suppressWarnings(     ackwards(bfi,       k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric",       pairs = "all", prune = "redundant",       redundancy_r = thr     )   )   sum(tidy(x, what = "nodes")$pruned) }) #> ℹ Redundancy pruning (|r| ≥ 0.8) flagged 8 nodes. #> ℹ Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #> ℹ Redundancy pruning (|r| ≥ 0.85) flagged 7 nodes. #> ℹ Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #> ℹ Redundancy pruning (|r| ≥ 0.9) flagged 6 nodes. #> ℹ Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #> ℹ Redundancy pruning (|r| ≥ 0.95) flagged 6 nodes. #> ℹ Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. data.frame(redundancy_r = thresholds, n_flagged = counts) #>   redundancy_r n_flagged #> 1         0.80         8 #> 2         0.85         7 #> 3         0.90         6 #> 4         0.95         6

what: The problem: which factor solution is right?
title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Anyone applied factor analysis faces dilemma: many factors extract? Parallel analysis might say 5; scree plot might suggest 3; reviewer might insist 1. solution seems describe data differently, tempting treat competing answers question. Goldberg (2006) reframed problem: different solutions competing — complementary. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance data, dimension along variables correlate. 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. two levels resolution describe underlying structure different vantage points, just satellite image street map describe city. bass-ackwards method makes relationship explicit. fits factor models every level 1 k, computes correlations factor scores across adjacent levels. correlations show : narrow factor inherits broad factor — lineage dimension move coarse fine resolution. factor splits — single broad factor correlates strongly two narrower factors fragmenting sub-dimensions. factor stable — factor correlates ~1.0 counterpart adjacent level essentially construct appearing two resolutions. result hierarchy map — visual numerical account factor structure data builds broad narrow. Note: descriptive, data-driven hierarchy, confirmatory hierarchical model like Schmid–Leiman higher-order SEM. -level correlations score correlations (algebraic equivalents), model parameters. See vignette("engines") use EFA ESEM instead PCA.

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: use 25-item Big Five Inventory psych package (psych::bfi). items measure five personality traits — Agreeableness (A1–A5), Conscientiousness (C1–C5), Extraversion (E1–E5), Neuroticism (N1–N5), Openness (O1–O5) — 6-point Likert scale, 2,800 participants. drop 364 cases missing item, leaving n = 2,436. real analysis might use pairwise deletion multiple imputation; na.omit() sufficient illustration.
code: library(ackwards)  bfi <- na.omit(psych::bfi[, 1:25]) dim(bfi) #> [1] 2436   25

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: hierarchy diagram centerpiece method. column represents one level (k = 1 left, k = 5 right). Arrows connect factor primary parent — factor level strongest correlation (|r|). Arrow thickness proportional |r|; color shows direction (blue = positive, orange = negative default); solid arrows indicate strong connections (|r| ≥ 0.6), dashed arrows show moderate ones (|r| ≥ 0.3).  Reading diagram left right tells story Big Five: k = 1: One broad factor. Given polychoric basis, captures shared variance across 25 items. k = 2: broad factor splits two: one become Extraversion/Agreeableness/Conscientiousness one become Neuroticism/Openness. k = 3: Three factors emerge, Neuroticism separating . k = 4: Agreeableness Extraversion begin differentiate. k = 5: canonical Big Five appear. factors labeled m{k}f{j} (level k, factor j). stable IDs used throughout object, m5f1 k = 5 refers factor loadings, edge table, factor scores, diagram.

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Factor scores place every observation factor every level. useful regression, clustering, downstream analysis want continuous summary latent dimension. augment(x, data = bfi) computes scores fly stored weight matrices appends data frame. score columns named .m{k}f{j} distinguish original variables. Scores standardized model-implied variance 1 — meaning scaling comes polychoric correlation matrix, raw data. practice empirical standard deviations close exactly 1 non-Pearson basis used; see ?augment.ackwards details. pure PCA Pearson correlations model-implied empirical variances agree exactly. need scores repeatedly (e.g., cross-validation loop), store fit time keep_scores = TRUE avoid re-applying weight matrices every call:

Factor scores are standardized using model-implied SDs from a "polychoric" #>   correlation matrix. #> ℹ The raw projection uses `scale(data)` (Pearson z-scores), but `score_var` #>   comes from the "polychoric" R. #> ℹ Empirical score SDs will differ from 1.0. what: Why Pearson underestimates for ordinal items
title: Ordinal Data: Polychoric Correlations and WLSMV
text: continuous latent trait sliced small number ordered categories, observed correlation two items always lower correlation underlying latent variables. coarse rating scale, larger attenuation. 5-point scale, Pearson correlations can 10–20% lower true latent correlations; 2-point (binary) scale, underestimate can exceed 30%. matters bass-ackwards analysis : Loadings attenuated. Factors appear weaker . -level edges attenuated. hierarchy looks flatter — factors nearly perfectly correlated across levels may appear moderately correlated. number factors underestimated. Attenuation compresses eigenvalue spectrum, selection criteria (parallel analysis, MAP) may suggest fewer factors truly exist.

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: suggest_k() returns multi-criterion object autoplot() method. produces three-panel ggplot2 diagnostic: Scree / Parallel Analysis — observed PC eigenvalues vs. PA-PC PA-FA simulated thresholds (95th percentile random data). MAP (minimize) — Velicer’s MAP criterion; star marks optimal k. VSS (maximize) — VSS-1 VSS-2 fit curves; stars mark optimum. EFAtools installed EFAtools::CD() ran successfully, dotted vertical line MAP panel indicates CD-suggested k.  Star-shaped markers (shape 8) identify recommended k criterion. Use consensus range across panels — converge k, strong evidence; spread 2–3 values, fit hierarchy depths compare interpretability.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis R ackwards implements Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method modern extensions mapping hierarchical structure multivariate data. core insight simple: instead asking many factors best describe data, ask solutions different levels resolution related one another. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance; 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. Bass-ackwards analysis traces broad factors split narrow ones — narrow factors redundant re-combinations broader ones. package supports three extraction engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM), polychoric correlations ordinal data, Forbes’s (2023) extension reveals skip-level connections flags redundant artefactual factors.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jmgirard/ackwards")

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: use 25-item Big Five Inventory (psych::bfi) running example. BFI items recorded 6-point ordinal scale, set cor = "polychoric".

what: Step 1 — Suggest a range of k
title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: suggest_k() runs five complementary criteria — two forms parallel analysis (PC FA basis), MAP, VSS, optionally Comparison Data — help choose upper bound hierarchy depth. criteria converge consensus range covers k = 5, consistent known Big Five structure instrument.
code: library(ackwards)  bfi <- na.omit(psych::bfi[, 1:25]) sk <- suggest_k(bfi) #> ℹ Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... #> ✔ Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... [211ms] #>  #> ℹ Running MAP and VSS... #> ✔ Running MAP and VSS... [93ms] #>  #> ℹ Running Comparison Data (CD)... #> ✔ Running Comparison Data (CD)... [8.6s] #>  sk #>  #> ── Factor / Component Count Suggestion (ackwards) ────────────────────────────── #> Variables: 25 #> n: 2,436 #> Basis: pearson #> Tested k: 1-8 #>  #> ── Criteria (k = 1-8) ── #>  #> k = 1: PA-PC ✔ PA-FA ✔ MAP 0.0249 VSS-1 0.5096 VSS-2 0.0000 CD ✔ #> k = 2: PA-PC ✔ PA-FA ✔ MAP 0.0189 VSS-1 0.5651 VSS-2 0.6560 CD ✔ #> k = 3: PA-PC ✔ PA-FA ✔ MAP 0.0175 VSS-1 0.5878 VSS-2 0.7343 CD ✔ #> k = 4: PA-PC ✔ PA-FA ✔ MAP 0.0157 VSS-1 0.6303* VSS-2 0.7809 CD ✔ #> k = 5: PA-PC ✔ PA-FA ✔ MAP 0.0146* VSS-1 0.5890 VSS-2 0.7944* CD ✔ #> k = 6: PA-PC - PA-FA ✔ MAP 0.0160 VSS-1 0.5646 VSS-2 0.7520 CD ✔ #> k = 7: PA-PC - PA-FA - MAP 0.0194 VSS-1 0.5617 VSS-2 0.7399 CD ✔ #> k = 8: PA-PC - PA-FA - MAP 0.0222 VSS-1 0.5449 VSS-2 0.7266 CD ✔* #>  #> ── Recommendations ── #>  #> • PA-PC: k <= 5 #> • PA-FA: k <= 6 #> • MAP: k = 5 #> • VSS-1: k = 4 #> • VSS-2: k = 5 #> • CD: k = 8 #> Consensus range: k = 4-8 #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Note: k in ackwards() is a maximum depth. Setting k one or two levels above the #> consensus to observe factor fragmentation is intentional. #> Caution: PA-PC tends to overextract; structures may not replicate (Forbes, #> 2023). PA-FA and CD are more conservative. Use the range.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: ackwards() fits factor models every level 1 k computes -level factor-score correlations define hierarchy.
code: x <- ackwards(bfi, k = 5, cor = "polychoric") x #>  #> ── Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) ─────────────────────────────────────────── #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 2,436 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> ── Levels ── #>  #> ✔ k = 1: 1 factor, 22.9% variance #> ✔ k = 2: 2 factors, 34.7% variance #> ✔ k = 3: 3 factors, 43.9% variance #> ✔ k = 4: 4 factors, 51.8% variance #> ✔ k = 5: 5 factors, 58.3% variance #>  #> ── Edges ── #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| ≥ 0.3 #> ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: data data frame numeric matrix observed variables (items columns, observations rows). Missing values handled via pairwise deletion computing R. Note: n_obs passed EFA engine always nrow(data); missingness effective per-correlation N may smaller, making chi-square / RMSEA / p-value slightly anti-conservative. k_max Maximum number factors/components extract. Required; use suggest_k() uncertain. Sets depth hierarchy: levels 1 k_max extracted. engine Extraction engine: "pca" (default), "efa", "esem". "esem" uses lavaan::efa() rotation-aware SEs per-level fit indices; recommended clinical/HiTOP workflow (Kim & Eaton, 2015; Forbush et al., 2024). Requires lavaan >= 0.6-13. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "polychoric". PCA/EFA, "polychoric" computes polychoric correlation matrix via psych::polychoric() (requires psych). ESEM, triggers WLSMV estimation via lavaan. cli warning emitted ordinal-looking columns detected cor "polychoric". fm Factor extraction method passed psych::fa(); used engine = "efa". One "minres" (default, robust OLS), "ml" (maximum likelihood, gives chi-square fit converges less reliably deep levels), "pa" (principal axis). Ignored engine = "pca". estimator Estimation method ESEM engine. NULL (default) auto-selects: "WLSMV" cor = "polychoric", "ML" otherwise. Pass explicitly override: "ULSMV" (unweighted WLS), "MLR" (robust ML). Ignored PCA EFA engines. align_signs Logical; sign-align factors primary-parent lineage? Default TRUE. keep_scores Logical; store factor scores result? Default FALSE (recomputable via augment.ackwards()). TRUE, per-observation scores stored x$scores named list n × k_j matrices, one per level, standardized real score SDs (see augment.ackwards()). keep_fits Logical; store raw engine fit objects? Default FALSE. TRUE, per-level fit objects (psych lavaan) stored x$fits named list indexed level. seed Integer seed stochastic engines (used PCA captured reproducibility metadata). Default NULL. pairs level pairs compute edges : "adjacent" (default, classic Goldberg — consecutive levels) "" (Forbes extension — every pair levels). "" reveals associations span multiple levels required redundancy pruning. Setting prune anything "none" automatically upgrades "" message. prune Character vector controlling Forbes-extension pruning. Default "none" (pruning). Options: "redundant" — identify chains factors connected primary-parent links |r| >= redundancy_r (optionally phi > redundancy_phi). Applies Forbes's (2023) retention rule: keep bottom node chain reaches level k (specific); keep top node otherwise. Pruning flag-: flagged nodes stay object pruned = TRUE prune_reason = "redundant" x$prune$nodes. "artefact" — compute Tucker's congruence coefficient (φ) cross-level factor pairs store x$prune$phi researcher inspection. factors auto-flagged; artefact identification requires judgment (Forbes, 2023; Wicherts et al., 2016). redundancy_r Scalar (0, 1]. Adjacent primary-parent |r| threshold redundancy chains. Default 0.9 (Forbes, 2023). redundancy_phi Scalar (0, 1] NULL (default). non-NULL, Tucker's φ must also exceed threshold link included redundancy chain (conjunctive redundancy_r). NULL means redundancy_r used. Recommended: 0.95 (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). cut_show Edges |r| >= cut_show flagged above_cut tidy() output. Default 0.3. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: object class "ackwards". See print.ackwards(), tidy.ackwards(), glance.ackwards(), augment.ackwards() output methods.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards
text: engine = "pca" — original Goldberg (2006) method; fastest; never fails converge; Waller (2007) algebra exact components. rotation = "varimax" — T'=T^-1 property orthogonal rotation enables closed-form W'RW edge algebra keeps within-level factors uncorrelated cross-level edges reflect hierarchical signal. Matches Goldberg (2006), Kim & Eaton (2015), Forbush et al. (2024). Varimax supported rotation; oblique rotation confound -level signal method's core output. cor = "pearson" — silent basis switching. items look ordinal (≤ 7 distinct integer values), cli warning suggest cor = "polychoric", available three engines. align_signs = TRUE — unaligned signs make output unreadable. Anchor: m1f1 oriented toward positive manifold; subsequent factor flipped edge primary parent positive. keep_scores = FALSE / keep_fits = FALSE — memory privacy. Scores O(n × Σk) often sensitive; raw engine fits can large. recomputable stored r matrix.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis — ackwards

title: Augment data with factor scores from an ackwards object — augment.ackwards
text: Score computation. Scores S = Z W / sqrt(score_var), Z = scale(data) (item z-scores), W per-level weight matrix stored object, sqrt(score_var) standardizes real score standard deviations (Invariant 1: never assume unit variance). PCA method "components"; EFA/ESEM "tenBerge". Missing data. Score projection applies weights row-wise propagates NAs listwise: observation least one missing item variable produce NA scores every level. differs fitting, uses pairwise-complete correlations. warning issued NA rows detected. Use na.omit(data) scoring NA rows unwanted. Data source. data supplied, scores always recomputed using stored weights — score new observations. data NULL keep_scores = TRUE set fit time, stored scores returned. neither available informative error raised.

title: Compute a layered layout for a bass-ackwards diagram — ba_layout
text: Top-pass – determines left--right order factors level. factor's ordinal rank |r|-weighted mean parents' ranks level , siblings share parent grouped together. Bottom-pass – assigns actual x coordinates. deepest level (level k) spread evenly; every upper-level factor placed simple mean x primary children: factor one primary child lands directly ; factor two primary children lands exactly halfway . title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis — suggest_k
text: Criteria computed: PA-PC (Horn 1965, PC basis) – parallel analysis principal-component eigenvalues. Compares observed eigenvalues random correlation matrices; suggests retaining components whose eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile chance. Tends overextract; treat upper bound. PA-FA (Horn 1965, FA basis) – parallel analysis using common-factor eigenvalues. conservative PA-PC better match EFA ESEM engines ackwards(). MAP (Velicer 1976) – Minimum Average Partial criterion. Finds k minimises average squared partial correlation remaining extracting k components. Usually conservative. VSS-1 / VSS-2 (Revelle & Rocklin 1979) – Simple Structure fit complexities 1 2. Finds k maximising fit simple loading structure. CD (Ruscio & Roche 2012; optional) – Comparison Data. Resamples observed item distributions generate comparison eigenvalue profiles; retains factors adding one longer improves RMSE beyond chance. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). PA (bases), MAP, VSS share correlation matrix ackwards(). CD operates raw data matrix directly (required resampling) skipped gracefully EFAtools installed.

Returns empty frame columns pruning applied (safe call unconditionally). x$prune$phi now includes abs_phi column alongside phi aid artefact screening sign alignment ambiguous non-primary cross-level factor pairs. Bug fix: match_parents() previously used clue::solve_LSAP (bipartite bijection), ill-posed bass-ackwards hierarchies — adjacent levels always children parents, padding row returned indices beyond nrow(E). Replaced greedy per-column argmax (apply(abs(E), 2, .max)), correct many--one parent assignment. clue removed Suggests.

title: ackwards 0.0.0.9000 (dev)
text: Added ESEM engine (method = "esem") using lavaan::efa() (requires lavaan >= 0.6-13) WLSMV ordinal estimation, rotation-aware SEs loadings_se, per-level fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR). Self-computed tenBerge weights keep compute_edges() algebra path ESEM EFA. Added cor = "polychoric" general basis option three engines. PCA/EFA use psych::polychoric() NPD smoothing; ESEM uses lavaan’s polychoric matrix (extracted WLSMV sampstat) ensure consistency fitted model. Added estimator argument ESEM engine (NULL auto-selects "WLSMV" cor = "polychoric", "ML" otherwise; user-overridable). Added loadings_se field §4 level contract (p × k matrix; NULL PCA EFA engines produce rotation-aware SEs). Updated ordinal-detection warning: longer fires cor = "polychoric" already set; message updated remove “future release” language. Added EFA engine (method = "efa") using psych::fa() tenBerge scoring weights, keeping W’RW algebra path compute_edges(). Convergence failures truncate hierarchy deepest converged level (warn + valid object). Heywood cases warn truncate. Orthogonal rotation now; fm controls extraction method ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). Added ackwards() implementing Goldberg’s (2006) bass-ackwards method PCA engine. Added compute_edges() computing -level factor-score correlations via W’RW algebra. Added print.ackwards(), tidy.ackwards(), glance.ackwards() output. Added ba_layout() computing Sugiyama-style barycenter layout bass-ackwards diagrams. Added autoplot.ackwards() plot.ackwards() ggplot2-based hierarchy diagrams (requires ggplot2; edges coloured sign, scaled |r|, solid/dashed strength). Added suggest_k() parallel analysis MAP (Velicer) criteria guide choice k. Fixed: scoring$basis PCA EFA engines now reflects actual cor= argument passed user rather always reporting "pearson". Fixed: EFA tenBerge weight fallback now labels scoring$method = "regression" .tenBerge_weights() fails psych regression weights used instead (Invariant 6). Added tidy(x, = "fit") returning one row per fit index × level (chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI, BIC EFA; eigenvalues PCA). Fixed: rotation = "cfQ" now errors immediately rather silently running oblimin. Fixed: scores = TRUE / keep_fits = TRUE now warn storage yet implemented. Fixed: print.ackwards() cut threshold now reads stored object rather param. Fixed: k = 1 now errors; least two levels required hierarchy. Fixed: kappa default corrected 1 / (2 * p) 1 / p, varimax-equivalent Crawford-Ferguson value (Crawford & Ferguson, 1970; Browne, 2001; Kim & Eaton, 2015). previous value (equamax-adjacent) silently wrong.
